title: P300 dataset from initial spot study
description: An auditory oddball event-related potential dataset from 13 healthy subjects designed to evaluate closed-loop optimization of stimulus onset asynchrony in brain-computer interfaces. The study employed 31-channel EEG recorded at 1000 Hz during presentation of target (1000 Hz) and non-target (500 Hz) tones at varying stimulus intervals, with machine learning-based classification using regularized linear discriminant analysis to identify individually optimal stimulation parameters.
methods_description: EEG data were acquired from 13 healthy subjects (mean age 22.7±1.64 years) using a 31-channel BrainProducts BrainAmp DC system at 1000 Hz sampling rate with nose reference and passive Ag/AgCl sensors. Subjects performed an auditory oddball task with target (1000 Hz) and non-target (500 Hz) tones presented at variable stimulus onset asynchronies (60-600 ms). The experiment consisted of an optimization phase (four strategies allocated 20 minutes each) and a validation phase (20 trials per stimulus onset asynchrony). Feature extraction used mean amplitudes across five time intervals (100-500 ms) and 31 channels, with classification performed using regularized linear discriminant analysis and 13-fold cross-validation.
